item.title: NL Soccer Players Win CAIS National Soccer Championship as Part of St. Andrew’s College
item.dc:creator: Richard Duggan
item.description:
Well, here’s somethin’ to crow about – a couple o’ young fellas from the Rock, Joe Duke from Mount Pearl and Nolan Martin from Conception Bay, went and helped their team, St. Andrew’s College, to sweep six games straight at the National Soccer Championships in Toronto. Snagged themselves some hardware, they did.
Joe, b’y, seems like he’s got a talent for ticklin' the twine, scoring four goals and two assists; got two of them when it really mattered in the semi-final. Their coach even said the lads were a big deal for the team. High praise, no doubt, eh?
I guess St. Andrew’s must’ve had some secret sauce. I mean, winning all the games? Maybe they had no snow delay practices like we do down here! All the same, good on ‘em – nice to see a couple of Newfoundlanders puttin’ their names out there... or in this case, on the scoreboard!
item.content:encoded:
Well, here’s somethin’ to crow about – a couple o’ young fellas from the Rock, Joe Duke from Mount Pearl and Nolan Martin from Conception Bay, went and helped their team, St. Andrew’s College, to sweep six games straight at the National Soccer Championships in Toronto. Snagged themselves some hardware, they did.
Joe, b’y, seems like he’s got a talent for ticklin' the twine, scoring four goals and two assists; got two of them when it really mattered in the semi-final. Their coach even said the lads were a big deal for the team. High praise, no doubt, eh?
I guess St. Andrew’s must’ve had some secret sauce. I mean, winning all the games? Maybe they had no snow delay practices like we do down here! All the same, good on ‘em – nice to see a couple of Newfoundlanders puttin’ their names out there... or in this case, on the scoreboard!

item.title: RNC Issues Warning After New, Highly Dangerous Drugs Found in Province
item.dc:creator: Richard Duggan
item.description:
Oh b'ys, you won't believe what just hit the Rock. The RNC is warnin' folks about some serious drugs found snuck into Newfoundland. Turns out we got carfentanil and methylfentanyl showin' up here for the first time. Now, these ain't your average pills—these things are used on big ol’ critters like horses. Just a grain or two of this stuff, and it's game over for humans, like lickin' salt but with deadly consequences.
Our strangely-familiar ol' methylfentanyl is apparently gonna knock your socks off too—imagine 400 to 600 times stronger than morphine. Like, wasn't fentanyl bad enough already? Now, these dodgy drugs are gettin' peddled as fentanyl but packin’ a helluva lot more punch.
The RNC reckon if you’re dabbling in these dangerous waters, you'd best have a naloxone kit handy. They're free, thanks to the Department of Health, bless 'em. And if things go sideways, remember the Good Samaritan Act protects ya when ya call for help. So, put the kettle on, and for the love of jigs dinner, make sure yer mates stay safe out there.
item.content:encoded:
Oh b'ys, you won't believe what just hit the Rock. The RNC is warnin' folks about some serious drugs found snuck into Newfoundland. Turns out we got carfentanil and methylfentanyl showin' up here for the first time. Now, these ain't your average pills—these things are used on big ol’ critters like horses. Just a grain or two of this stuff, and it's game over for humans, like lickin' salt but with deadly consequences.
Our strangely-familiar ol' methylfentanyl is apparently gonna knock your socks off too—imagine 400 to 600 times stronger than morphine. Like, wasn't fentanyl bad enough already? Now, these dodgy drugs are gettin' peddled as fentanyl but packin’ a helluva lot more punch.
The RNC reckon if you’re dabbling in these dangerous waters, you'd best have a naloxone kit handy. They're free, thanks to the Department of Health, bless 'em. And if things go sideways, remember the Good Samaritan Act protects ya when ya call for help. So, put the kettle on, and for the love of jigs dinner, make sure yer mates stay safe out there.

item.title: NL Drag Queen Competing on Canada’s Drag Race
item.dc:creator: Richard Duggan
item.description:
Well now, b’ys, what do ya think about this one, eh? Tara Nova, one o’ them drag queens from St. John’s, is takin’ her talent out from under the bridge and onto the national telly. She’s gonna be struttin’ her stuff on Season 5 of Canada’s Drag Race, streamin’ on Crave. Good on her, I s’pose, though I’d like to see them try and portray “creative art” while dodgin’ potholes down Water Street, right?
Anyways, she’s only the second from Newfoundland and Labrador to get on there. The first was Irma Gerd, who made a splash back in Season 3. The show kicks off on November 21st, so mark your calendars, if yer into that sorta thing. Let’s just hope Tara don’t end up lipsyncin' for her life against some Ontarian queen, eh? Cause we know we’d be rootin’ for the hometown hero, go on, by.
item.content:encoded:
Well now, b’ys, what do ya think about this one, eh? Tara Nova, one o’ them drag queens from St. John’s, is takin’ her talent out from under the bridge and onto the national telly. She’s gonna be struttin’ her stuff on Season 5 of Canada’s Drag Race, streamin’ on Crave. Good on her, I s’pose, though I’d like to see them try and portray “creative art” while dodgin’ potholes down Water Street, right?
Anyways, she’s only the second from Newfoundland and Labrador to get on there. The first was Irma Gerd, who made a splash back in Season 3. The show kicks off on November 21st, so mark your calendars, if yer into that sorta thing. Let’s just hope Tara don’t end up lipsyncin' for her life against some Ontarian queen, eh? Cause we know we’d be rootin’ for the hometown hero, go on, by.

item.title: Bridge on Burin Peninsula Highway Down to One Lane for Extended Period
item.dc:creator: Richard Duggan
item.description:
Well, b’ys, looks like the bridge down on the Burin Peninsula Highway is gonna be a right pickle till next year. They figured the Red Harbour West River Bridge was an easy fix, but turns out it’s like trying to patch a leaky dory with a roll of duct tape – more work than anyone thought. Now they got ‘er down to one lane with traffic lights up, trying to keep things safe what with it being at the bottom of a hill, snow, ice, and all that good stuff.
MHA Jeff Dwyer says folks need to hold their horses and be patient, but try telling that to a Newfoundlander stuck in a jam, eh? Said the Baine Harbour Fire Department has been busy as hens in a feed store, already had 21 callouts this year, 19 of 'em from car accidents. If I were them, I'd be looking to maybe get a frequent responder card or something at this rate! Anyway, here's hoping it gets sorted sooner rather than later, 'cause there's only so much road rage one can take.
item.content:encoded:
Well, b’ys, looks like the bridge down on the Burin Peninsula Highway is gonna be a right pickle till next year. They figured the Red Harbour West River Bridge was an easy fix, but turns out it’s like trying to patch a leaky dory with a roll of duct tape – more work than anyone thought. Now they got ‘er down to one lane with traffic lights up, trying to keep things safe what with it being at the bottom of a hill, snow, ice, and all that good stuff.
MHA Jeff Dwyer says folks need to hold their horses and be patient, but try telling that to a Newfoundlander stuck in a jam, eh? Said the Baine Harbour Fire Department has been busy as hens in a feed store, already had 21 callouts this year, 19 of 'em from car accidents. If I were them, I'd be looking to maybe get a frequent responder card or something at this rate! Anyway, here's hoping it gets sorted sooner rather than later, 'cause there's only so much road rage one can take.

item.title: Tax and Red Tape Cuts on Table as PC’s Announce Policies Aimed at Businesses
item.dc:creator: Richard Duggan
item.description:
Well b'ys, looks like Tony Wakeham is at it again, trying to cozy up to the business folk with his newest election promise. He’s calling businesses the "backbone" of our economy—no surprise there—but he's ranting about how they’re getting bogged down by high costs and more red tape than a Christmas gift. He reckons his bunch will sort this mess out by giving taxes and fees a once-over, scrap that pesky payroll tax on full-timers, and cut through the mountains of red tape like a snowblower in February.
Wakeham’s been shouting about how we lead the country in red tape, which is a real dubious honor if ya ask me. His plan? Be the champs at reducing it instead. He even gave an example about business naming where ya gotta fork out $10 just to hold a name and then cough up another $300 to actually register it. Double the effort for double the headache, right?
Seems like Wakeham’s really trying to trim the fat, but we’ll see if this cuttin’ through the nonsense gets him anywhere or if it’s all talk to gain some votes. Guess time will tell, eh?
item.content:encoded:
Well b'ys, looks like Tony Wakeham is at it again, trying to cozy up to the business folk with his newest election promise. He’s calling businesses the "backbone" of our economy—no surprise there—but he's ranting about how they’re getting bogged down by high costs and more red tape than a Christmas gift. He reckons his bunch will sort this mess out by giving taxes and fees a once-over, scrap that pesky payroll tax on full-timers, and cut through the mountains of red tape like a snowblower in February.
Wakeham’s been shouting about how we lead the country in red tape, which is a real dubious honor if ya ask me. His plan? Be the champs at reducing it instead. He even gave an example about business naming where ya gotta fork out $10 just to hold a name and then cough up another $300 to actually register it. Double the effort for double the headache, right?
Seems like Wakeham’s really trying to trim the fat, but we’ll see if this cuttin’ through the nonsense gets him anywhere or if it’s all talk to gain some votes. Guess time will tell, eh?

item.title: Justin Trudeau Not Stepping Down Despite Request From Party Detractors
item.dc:creator: Richard Duggan
item.description:
Well, b'ys, it looks like Trudeau's decided to stick 'round like the last snow in June. Despite some folks in his own party tryin' to give him the ol' heave-ho with that letter, he's not budging before the end of October. Reckon he came outta a big meeting claimin' everything's all sunshine and rainbows in the party, even if some of 'em seem to be eyein' the door.
He’s saying they’re havin’ some “robust conversations,” which we all know is code for the kind of chat that might leave a few bruises. But hey, who doesn't love a good bit of drama on the mainland, right?
Oh, and in true Trudeau fashion, he couldn't resist throwin’ a bit of shade at the Conservatives. He brings up one of their folks takin' a free trip to Florida to hang out with some far-right folks and another one dinin' with neo-Nazis. Kinda spicy for a politician’s buffet, don’t ya think? And then there's Poilievre flappin' about national security without a clearance – that's like wonderin' why the fish all buggered off while you’re haulin’ a boat with a hole in the bottom.
So there you have it. Trudeau’s staying put, and it looks like it’ll be more political mud-slingin’ as usual. But on the bright side, at least it keeps us entertained while we're waitin' for the fish to run, right?
item.content:encoded:
Well, b'ys, it looks like Trudeau's decided to stick 'round like the last snow in June. Despite some folks in his own party tryin' to give him the ol' heave-ho with that letter, he's not budging before the end of October. Reckon he came outta a big meeting claimin' everything's all sunshine and rainbows in the party, even if some of 'em seem to be eyein' the door.
He’s saying they’re havin’ some “robust conversations,” which we all know is code for the kind of chat that might leave a few bruises. But hey, who doesn't love a good bit of drama on the mainland, right?
Oh, and in true Trudeau fashion, he couldn't resist throwin’ a bit of shade at the Conservatives. He brings up one of their folks takin' a free trip to Florida to hang out with some far-right folks and another one dinin' with neo-Nazis. Kinda spicy for a politician’s buffet, don’t ya think? And then there's Poilievre flappin' about national security without a clearance – that's like wonderin' why the fish all buggered off while you’re haulin’ a boat with a hole in the bottom.
So there you have it. Trudeau’s staying put, and it looks like it’ll be more political mud-slingin’ as usual. But on the bright side, at least it keeps us entertained while we're waitin' for the fish to run, right?

item.title: Sea-Doos and Boat Trailer Stolen from Deer Lake Home
item.dc:creator: Richard Duggan
item.description:
Well b'ys, you won't believe this one. Couple of eejits down in Deer Lake decided they'd just casually help themselves to two Sea-Doos and a boat trailer from some poor soul's yard on Garden Road, would ya look at that, hey? Happened at the crack of dawn around 6 o'clock Wednesday mornin'.
Now, the RCMP are lookin' to get the rigs back. Apparently, a white SUV was trottin' around town with the stolen goods in tow after the grab. Sure, if you're gonna be a crook, ya might as well be seen drivin' the evidence 'round town, right?
So if anyone knowin' anything 'bout this is asked to give a shout to the RCMP or Crime Stoppers. Who knows, maybe one of the b'ys down at Tim's knows somethin'. Let’s just hope those Sea-Doos find their way back home before they're mistaken for icebergs.
item.content:encoded:
Well b'ys, you won't believe this one. Couple of eejits down in Deer Lake decided they'd just casually help themselves to two Sea-Doos and a boat trailer from some poor soul's yard on Garden Road, would ya look at that, hey? Happened at the crack of dawn around 6 o'clock Wednesday mornin'.
Now, the RCMP are lookin' to get the rigs back. Apparently, a white SUV was trottin' around town with the stolen goods in tow after the grab. Sure, if you're gonna be a crook, ya might as well be seen drivin' the evidence 'round town, right?
So if anyone knowin' anything 'bout this is asked to give a shout to the RCMP or Crime Stoppers. Who knows, maybe one of the b'ys down at Tim's knows somethin'. Let’s just hope those Sea-Doos find their way back home before they're mistaken for icebergs.

item.title: Ottawa Reducing Immigration Numbers for Next Three Years
item.dc:creator: Richard Duggan
item.description:
Ah, buddy, let me tell ya what's goin' on now with Trudeau and his crew up in Ottawa. So, they've decided to put the brakes on bringin' in new folks from away for a bit, eh? Apparently, they've been playin’ musical chairs with the numbers n’ they reckon they need to slow down. He's talkin' about makin' sure there's enough for health care and housing before inviting more people over. I s'pose that's fair enough, but don't they always say that after the horse has bolted?
Turns out, things got a bit out of whack, especially during the pandemic when they were desperate for workers. I suppose they were opening the doors wider than a barn in a nor'easter, weren't they? Trudeau himself is ownin' up to the fact they didn't nail the balance, and they're cuttin' back from 500,000 immigrants to 395,000 in 2025, and only 365,000 by 2027. I guess they’re lookin’ to manage the madness a bit better, but who knows if they'll pull it off.
They say they're also plannin' to turn more of the folks already here on temporary work permits into permanent residents. Kind of like sayin', "Since you've been here helpin' us out, we might as well keep ya." Ah well, let’s just hope they get it sorted before it turns into another kerfuffle!
item.content:encoded:
Ah, buddy, let me tell ya what's goin' on now with Trudeau and his crew up in Ottawa. So, they've decided to put the brakes on bringin' in new folks from away for a bit, eh? Apparently, they've been playin’ musical chairs with the numbers n’ they reckon they need to slow down. He's talkin' about makin' sure there's enough for health care and housing before inviting more people over. I s'pose that's fair enough, but don't they always say that after the horse has bolted?
Turns out, things got a bit out of whack, especially during the pandemic when they were desperate for workers. I suppose they were opening the doors wider than a barn in a nor'easter, weren't they? Trudeau himself is ownin' up to the fact they didn't nail the balance, and they're cuttin' back from 500,000 immigrants to 395,000 in 2025, and only 365,000 by 2027. I guess they’re lookin’ to manage the madness a bit better, but who knows if they'll pull it off.
They say they're also plannin' to turn more of the folks already here on temporary work permits into permanent residents. Kind of like sayin', "Since you've been here helpin' us out, we might as well keep ya." Ah well, let’s just hope they get it sorted before it turns into another kerfuffle!

item.title: Government Hopeful New ECE Benefits Package Will Boost Staffing
item.dc:creator: Sara Strickland
item.description:
Well, me ol' cock, it seems like John Haggie is trying to tackle the shortage of early childhood educators over there. They've cooked up a benefits plan where the gov'ment pays 60% for folks workin' in regulated centers. Workers and their bosses gotta cover the rest of it. Started this on the first of October, b'y.
Haggie figures this might keep more people stickin' around and maybe get some new blood into the gig too. Lord knows we need more spots for the little bay b'ys and girls — about 3,000 more spots, he says. How about that?
Of course, it's nice to see 'em tryin' to sweeten the pot for these workers. But let's be honest, if this whole thing goes under like Nan's boat, who's gonna get the blame, eh? All we can hope is that Haggie's big plan floats better than a dory full of cod. We'll see how she goes, I s'pose.
item.content:encoded:
Well, me ol' cock, it seems like John Haggie is trying to tackle the shortage of early childhood educators over there. They've cooked up a benefits plan where the gov'ment pays 60% for folks workin' in regulated centers. Workers and their bosses gotta cover the rest of it. Started this on the first of October, b'y.
Haggie figures this might keep more people stickin' around and maybe get some new blood into the gig too. Lord knows we need more spots for the little bay b'ys and girls — about 3,000 more spots, he says. How about that?
Of course, it's nice to see 'em tryin' to sweeten the pot for these workers. But let's be honest, if this whole thing goes under like Nan's boat, who's gonna get the blame, eh? All we can hope is that Haggie's big plan floats better than a dory full of cod. We'll see how she goes, I s'pose.

item.title: Kansas Man Killed in Driftwood Inn Fire Came to Newfoundland on Dream Hunting Trip
item.dc:creator: Sara Strickland
item.description:
Oh, b'ys, this one is a right tragic story from the Driftwood Inn. So this fella from Kansas, Eugene Earl Spoon was his name, came all the way up to Newfoundland for his dream moose hunt, eh? Seventy-seven, he was, and fresh from the heartbreak of losing his missus last Christmas. Poor man never did get that moose though, ‘cause he got caught up in a fire at the inn and his remains were found afterward. Right hard luck, that.
Now, you'd think the province and everyone involved would have more to say, but nah, they're all zipped up like they're keeping a prize catch secret. Meanwhile, some people are wondering if the fire alarms did their job, or if this mistake could've been avoided. And big kudos to this lady, Melinda Sharpe, who was going around banging on doors to get folks up. Probably saved a few with her racket.
And now the whole mess is in the hands of the exams and the fire investigation folks, but I suppose we'll be waiting on bated breath to hear what went wrong. I'll tell you one thing, with all the finger-pointing at the moment, it’s a bit like trying to find a needle in a haystack. Suppose that's government for ya!
item.content:encoded:
Oh, b'ys, this one is a right tragic story from the Driftwood Inn. So this fella from Kansas, Eugene Earl Spoon was his name, came all the way up to Newfoundland for his dream moose hunt, eh? Seventy-seven, he was, and fresh from the heartbreak of losing his missus last Christmas. Poor man never did get that moose though, ‘cause he got caught up in a fire at the inn and his remains were found afterward. Right hard luck, that.
Now, you'd think the province and everyone involved would have more to say, but nah, they're all zipped up like they're keeping a prize catch secret. Meanwhile, some people are wondering if the fire alarms did their job, or if this mistake could've been avoided. And big kudos to this lady, Melinda Sharpe, who was going around banging on doors to get folks up. Probably saved a few with her racket.
And now the whole mess is in the hands of the exams and the fire investigation folks, but I suppose we'll be waiting on bated breath to hear what went wrong. I'll tell you one thing, with all the finger-pointing at the moment, it’s a bit like trying to find a needle in a haystack. Suppose that's government for ya!
